title: Parent Support Groups * Autism Spectrum Disorder - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Child Care & Parenting > Parent Support Groups * Autism Spectrum Disorder 6 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Autism Spectrum Disorder Disabilities Issues Hispanic/Latino Community Mothers Perinatal Depression Substance Use Disorder Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF AUTISM SOCIETY OF OREGON SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of virtual and in-person support groups. 9320 SW Barbur Boulevard Suite 240 Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: Anyone in the austism community, along with their parents, caregivers, and supporters Hours: Varies by group Email (503) 636-1676 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGEWAY HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers developmental therapies and supports including in-home occupational and speech therapy, social groups, classes, and tutoring. Also offers parent support groups, parent and child activity groups, parenting classes, and specialized information and referral. 555 E 15th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Families with children living with autism and related disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 345-0805 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIRST STEPS CENTER FOR AUTISM AND DISABILITIES SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support groups, social opportunities, events, workshops, and resources for individuals and families experiencing neurodivergence. Accepts volunteers. 228 S Main Avenue Warrenton, OR 97146 Eligibility: Teen Group: Neurodivergent teens, ages 13-19 Adult Group: Neurodivergent adults, age 18 and older Parents and Caregivers Group: Parents and caregivers of neurodivergent individuals Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm Teen Group: Friday 7pm Adult Group: 3rd Thursday of the month 6pm Parents and Caregivers Group: 2nd Thursday of the month 6pm Email (503) 861-8104 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK MID-OREGON REGION OFFICE REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 4263 Commercial Street SE Suite 300 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays.
